name: Classic Meatloaf
tagline: Beef-pork loaf with glazy ketchup top and crisp edges

hero.alt: Sliced meatloaf with caramelized ketchup glaze
hero.caption: meatloaf — glazed top, juicy slice

steps.title: Soak the panade
steps.detail: Stir breadcrumbs into milk in a large bowl; rest 5 minutes until completely soft. This is the key to tender meatloaf.
steps.tip: Without soaked bread, you have meatloaf-shaped meatball.

steps.title: Mix gently
steps.detail: Add beef, pork, egg, onion, garlic, Worcestershire, salt, pepper. Mix with hands until just combined — overmixing = tough.
steps.tip: Pinch off and microwave a small piece to taste-check seasoning before baking.

steps.title: Shape and glaze
steps.detail: Form into a free-form loaf on a lined sheet pan. Whisk ketchup with brown sugar and slather over the top.
steps.tip: Free-form on a sheet pan beats a loaf pan — more crust, less greasy.

steps.title: Bake to 160°F
steps.detail: Bake 350°F for 55–65 minutes until internal temp reads 160°F (71°C). Rest 10 minutes before slicing.
steps.tip: Cutting hot floods the slice with juice — rest is mandatory.

healthFacts: Mixing beef and pork balances flavor and fat — pure beef is too lean and dense.
Soaked breadcrumbs (panade) hold moisture and prevent a tough loaf.
